name: GDELT Project API
what_it_does: Provides open access to the world's largest database of global news events, sentiment, themes, people, and locations derived from global media monitoring since 1979.
use_cases: Large-scale academic research on global media trends
Sentiment and tone analysis across global media
Geographic mapping of news events and themes
not_for: Real-time news applications requiring low latency
Production apps needing guaranteed uptime SLAs
best_when: Conducting research or analysis on global media patterns, geopolitical events, or social trends at scale where cost is a constraint and data richness matters more than timeliness.
avoid_when: You need real-time news, clean article text, guaranteed uptime, or simple query patterns — GDELT's complexity and academic focus make it unsuitable for mainstream app development.

auth.notes: No authentication required; fully open API with no API key needed

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited API access; BigQuery queries billed by Google at standard rates
pricing.paid_tiers: None for API; BigQuery usage costs apply for large-scale queries
pricing.notes: API itself is free and open; bulk BigQuery analysis may incur Google Cloud costs

performance.rate_limits: Unofficial; no documented rate limits but aggressive use may be throttled

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Minimal error responses; documentation is academic in style and inconsistently maintained
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only historical data; all requests are naturally idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: No SLA; service can be slow or unavailable without warning
Data format is complex CAMEO event coding — agents need domain knowledge to interpret
Not real-time: data typically delayed 15-30 minutes at best
No formal rate limits documented — aggressive agent polling may get throttled without notice
Documentation scattered across academic papers and informal blog posts

security_score_components.security_notes: No auth at all — open data; no secrets to manage which is both a strength (no credential leakage) and a risk (no access control); HTTPS available but not always enforced
